content.title: DevFlow Internal Alpha
content.body:
**DevFlow Internal Alpha** — Q2 2026 milestone for the DevFlow product family.

**Goal:** Get DevFlow components to internal-alpha state for AOCyber team dogfooding. Foundation for the broader DevFlow Internal Beta (Q4 2026) and Public Launch (Q3 2027) milestones.

**Scope spans these repos:**
- `devflow` (Go CLI/daemon) — local dev platform
- `devflow-claude` — AI workflow orchestration + program-aware coordination layer
- `eden-libs` — shared SDK consumed across products

**Tracks to:** Product Roadmap project — DevFlow / Q2 2026 / In Progress

**Sub-issues:** see linked items below. The list is the work that has to land for internal alpha to be real.

content.title: [Roadmap] DevFlow Claude — AI Workflow Orchestration + Program-Aware Coordination Layer
content.body:
DevFlow Claude is the Claude Code plugin layer for AI-assisted development across the AO-Cyber-Systems org. Sibling to the `devflow` Go CLI/daemon (local dev platform); both serve the broader DevFlow product family.

**Scope:** AI workflow orchestration (skills, hooks, MCP integration, planning state, intent model) **+** program-aware coordination layer for cross-repo work.

**Architectural principle:** Plan org-aware, execute repo-focused. Planning consults org state (sibling repos, eden-libs reuse, this Product Roadmap project) to surface overlap, duplication risk, and shared-service opportunities. Execution stays a local heads-down loop with at most a thin async preamble.

## Active milestones

### v1.1 — DevFlow Coordination Layer (Q2 2026, in flight)
Foundation for program-aware coordination. 9 objectives:
1. GitHub coordination layer (frontmatter conventions + resolver service)
2. Cross-worktree session telemetry + heartbeat schema
3. Planning-time org awareness (df:plan-objective surveys siblings + eden-libs + Product Roadmap)
4. Duplicate-work detection + 4-option resolution flow
5. Initiative context layer (disk projection of GitHub Epics)
6. Unified df:check-todos (urgency-lane view across local + GH + heartbeats + initiatives)
7. df:handoff watcher daemon — non-disruptive command handoff (Approach B; in-flight on `feature/seamless-handoff` successor branch)
8. Program-aware TUI viewer (parallel sessions + program tree)
9. Roadmap ↔ disk reconciliation

### v1.2 — Handoff Watcher PTY + Polish (Q3-Q4 2026)
- Node-pty backend for the watcher daemon → TTY-interactive auth (`doctl auth init`, `gh auth login`, `sudo`, `gpg`) works end-to-end
- OS notifications, auto-launch service, multi-project watching, status-line indicator
- Cross-shell support (fish, nushell, pwsh)
- Bidirectional GitHub sync
- Workflow-impediment fixes (df-tools branch-awareness, project-hygiene tooling)

## Tracks to

- DevFlow product line in the org Product Roadmap project (`PVT_kwDODwqLrc4BRsOP`)
- Parent epic: DevFlow Internal Alpha (Q2 2026)
- Sibling tracks: `aodex#33` (Go Backend Migration), `aosentry#20` (Commercial Launch + Go Migration), `aodex-flutter#2` (Flutter Client), `eden-libs/16-ai-platform-sdk` (shared SDK)

## Repo

`AO-Cyber-Systems/devflow-claude` — Claude Code plugin (`devflow@aocyber`) installed via `/plugin marketplace add AO-Cyber-Systems/devflow-claude`. Distinct from `AO-Cyber-Systems/devflow` (Go CLI/daemon — local dev platform).

Planning state lives at `.planning/` (PROJECT.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md, research/, objectives/) on the active milestone branch.

nodes.text: [Roadmap] DevFlow Claude — AI Workflow Orchestration + Program-Aware Coordination Layer

content.title: [v1.1-1] GitHub coordination layer (frontmatter + resolver)
content.body:
**Foundation for v1.1.** Frontmatter conventions (parent_issue, github_issue, org_milestone) on OBJECTIVE.md and TRD.md. Resolver service (`df-tools resolver context`) walking objective → repo `[Roadmap]` issue → org Product milestone → sibling repo activity → initiative.

Design: `.planning/research/org-context-resolver.md` (exists on `feature/v1.1-coordination`).

Build order: foundation → GH integration → heartbeat integration → consumer wiring (planner agent, SessionStart hook, df:check-todos all switch to using resolver).

This issue is the prerequisite for objectives #2 (heartbeat) and #3 (planning awareness), which together depend on the resolver as their data layer.

content.title: [v1.1-2] Cross-worktree session telemetry + heartbeat
content.body:
Hook-driven heartbeat schema (session_id, project, branch, github_issue, objective, job, files_touched, files_planned, state, blocked_on_user). Storage: dedicated lightweight git repo recommended (alternatives: comments-on-issue, Project custom field).

Lifecycle hooks: SessionStart write-initial, df:plan-objective/build/execute updates, file-edit debounced updates, blocked-on-user transitions, Stop write-done + auto-prune.

Foundation for #4 (duplicate detection), #6 (df:check-todos), #7 (df:handoff status), #8 (TUI viewer).

Design: `.planning/research/cross-session-coordination.md`.

content.title: [v1.1-3] Planning-time org awareness
content.body:
Extend df:research-objective + df:plan-objective: at planning start, call `df-tools resolver context` to load sibling repo activity, eden-libs reuse opportunities, and the org Product Roadmap entry for the parent epic.

Output as a Cross-Repo Considerations section in CONTEXT.md/PLAN.md, influencing TRD generation. Surfaces:
- Misfile detection ("this objective doesn't fit this repo")
- Shared-service flag ("two repos about to add the same thing — extract to eden-libs?")
- Open-question carryover from active initiatives
- Sibling overlap warnings (advisory; #4 handles strong matches)

Depends on #1 (resolver foundation).

content.title: [v1.1-4] Duplicate-work detection + resolution flow
content.body:
Plan-time check (in df:plan-objective) and execute-time check (in df:execute-objective) compare the candidate work against active heartbeats from #2.

Signals: file overlap (strong), symbol overlap (strong), same github_issue (hard), semantic intent similarity (advisory).

Resolution flow when fired: Merge / Defer / Coordinate / Proceed-anyway. Recorded in both sessions' heartbeats.

Per autopilot-after-setup feedback: don't gate every plan on confirmation — only strong matches block, weak matches print advisory.

Depends on #1 + #2.

content.title: [v1.1-5] Initiative context layer
content.body:
Disk projection of GitHub Epics at `~/.claude/devflow/initiatives/<name>.md`. Schema: name, github_epic, status, key_repos, summary, why, open questions, recent context. Editable by hand; `df:initiatives sync` pulls Epic body → disk file.

Planner reads matching initiatives by key_repos at plan time; includes Why + Open Questions in the planning context; populates parent_epic frontmatter.

Independent of heartbeat — can land in parallel with #2.

content.title: [v1.1-6] Unified df:check-todos (urgency-lane view)
content.body:
Replace per-repo `df:check-todos` with a multi-source aggregator covering:
- Local `.planning/todos/` (current + recently-touched repos)
- GitHub Issues (assigned, mentioned, review-requested across all 5 primary repos)
- Active heartbeats (own paused/blocked sessions; teammates' sessions where I'm listed as blocker)
- Initiative open-questions

Output grouped by urgency lane (Blocked on me / Resume in flight / New since yesterday / Reminders / Background), each item with provenance.

Depends on #2 (heartbeats) + GH integration from #1.

content.title: [v1.1-7] df:handoff watcher daemon (seamless handoff)
content.body:
**In flight on `handoff-completion-work` branch.** Implementation done; PR pending.

Local daemon (`devflow-watch`) executes interactive + shell-flow commands in user's interactive shell so Claude continues executing without paste interruptions. Approach B from `docs/PROPOSAL-handoff-watcher.md`.

Status: 8 of 8 TRDs complete (state lib, allowlist, daemon CLI, shell session, route-results hook, gate-interactive update, e2e tests, skill+docs). 293/293 tests pass. Demonstrated end-to-end with doctl auth init.

Known v1.1 limitation: TTY-required prompts (doctl/gh auth login/sudo) still need paste fallback because dispatch uses stdio pipes. PTY backend is the v1.2 headline (`feature/v1.1-coordination` ROADMAP.md documents this).

content.title: [v1.1-8] Program-aware TUI viewer
content.body:
Live viewer rendering parallel Claude sessions + their position in the org tree (parent epic, milestone, sibling progress). Read-only; doesn't gate execution. tmux-pane friendly.

Polls `df-tools resolver heartbeats --active` every ~5s. Groups sessions by parent_issue so the program tree is visible, not just a flat list.

Depends on #1 + #2.

content.title: [v1.1-9] Roadmap ↔ disk reconciliation
content.body:
`df:sync-roadmap` reconciles ROADMAP.md checkboxes ↔ on-disk SUMMARY.md presence.

Today's drift example (caught during this milestone's planning): `devflow-claude/feature/agent-control-plane` had ROADMAP showing all 14 TRDs of objective 01 as `[x]` while only 6 SUMMARY.md files existed on disk. Mechanical to detect; manual to fix today.

Independent of runtime layer — can land any time.

content.title: [v1.1-0] Refine (kind, work) defaults table from codebase evidence + testing-strategy matrix
content.body:
**Objective 0: Refine (kind, work) defaults table from codebase evidence**

**Goal:** Refine the 42-cell `(kind, work)` defaults table at `plugins/devflow/devflow/references/defaults-table.md` to match observed AOCyber codebase reality, codify the user's CLAUDE.md TDD Playbook as structured fields in the `intent.cjs` resolver, and add a parallel `references/testing-strategy.md` testing-levels matrix (folds in #7). The resolver becomes the enforcement mechanism for the TDD Playbook, not a parallel set of guidelines.

**Status:** 6/6 TRDs done, current wave 4, last commit 61d4832

**Success criteria:**
- [x] SC-1: `defaults-table.md` reflects 27 changed cells + 5 new column headers; the file format remains valid YAML reference doc parseable by the planner.
- [x] SC-2: `intent.cjs` resolver emits the 5 new structured fields + 3 anti-pattern constraints; provenance is reported per field (`table` / `user_playbook` / `trd_override` / `objective_override`).
- [x] SC-3: Planner agent reads new fields and emits corresponding TRD sections (test-list checklist, fixture-builder task, wrong-tenant assertion in test list, outside-in TRD ordering when applicable).
- [x] SC-4: CLAUDE.md absorption maps all 6 TDD Playbook habits cleanly to 5 structured fields + 1 freeform directive.
- [x] SC-5: `references/testing-strategy.md` exists with the layer×tool×stack matrix from #7 (unit → integration → AI exploratory → Maestro → visual) plus the Flutter-web semantics gotcha, codegen discipline, and platform routing paragraphs.
- [x] SC-6: Planner consults testing-strategy.md when emitting verification commands; layer→tool routing reflects detected stack.
- [x] SC-7: Existing PROJECT.md / OBJECTIVE.md / TRD.md files don't break — migration path documented and validated against the existing `01-handoff-watcher` objective directory.
- [x] SC-8: Critical sequencing constraint honored: TRD 01 (table) and TRD 02 (resolver schema) ship in different waves/commits so the schema has soak time before #12 / #13 lock onto it.
- [x] SC-9: `df-tools intent resolve --objective <fixture>` round-trip succeeds on a fixture project containing all 6 kinds × 7 work types and exercises the `multi_tenant_required` path.
- [x] SC-10: `npm test` (Node native test runner) passes; new TDD-tagged TRDs (02, 04, 05) ship `test:` commits preceding their `feat:` commits per the user's TDD Playbook.

**TRDs:**
- [x] 00-01-defaults-table-update-TRD.md — Update defaults-table.md — 27 changed cells + 5 new column headers
- [x] 00-02-resolver-schema-TRD.md — Extend intent.cjs resolver — emit 5 new fields + 3 anti-pattern constraints with provenance
- [x] 00-03-planner-agent-update-TRD.md — Update planner agent — read 5 new fields + emit corresponding TRD sections + consult testing-strategy.md
- [x] 00-04-claude-md-absorption-TRD.md — Map all 6 TDD Playbook habits to 5 structured fields + 1 freeform directive
- [x] 00-05-migration-provenance-TRD.md — Migration validation + per-field provenance integration test
- [x] 00-06-testing-strategy-doc-TRD.md — Author references/testing-strategy.md (closes #7)

_Tracked by [DevFlow](https://github.com/AO-Cyber-Systems/devflow-claude). Source of truth: `.planning/objectives/00-refine-defaults-table/` in this repo._

nodes.text: [v1.1-0] Refine (kind, work) defaults table from codebase evidence + testing-strategy matrix
